---
title: "Project Home"
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    body-classes: page-home
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
live_shiny_app_url <- "https://zrwang2025mitbsmuedusg.shinyapps.io/singapore-tourism-va/"
shiny_app_url <- trimws(Sys.getenv("SHINY_APP_URL", unset = live_shiny_app_url))
if (!nzchar(shiny_app_url)) {
  shiny_app_url <- live_shiny_app_url
}
```

::: {.landing-home}
::: {.landing-hero}
::: {.landing-hero-copy}
<div class="landing-eyebrow">Singapore Tourism Recovery | Visual Analytics Project</div>

# Singapore Tourism Recovery

This website presents the project as one connected analytical workflow. Visual Analysis establishes source-market structure. Clustering compares recovery trajectories across countries. Forecasting projects the selected series forward and interprets that projection with tourism-performance context. The live Shiny app, the PDF user guide, and the poster all point to the same three-module structure.

::: {.landing-actions}
[Open Live Shiny App](`r shiny_app_url`){target="_blank" .btn-home-primary}
[Open User Guide PDF](./user-guide.html){.btn-home-secondary}
[View Poster](./poster.html){.btn-home-secondary}
:::

::: {.landing-meta}
<div><span>Modules</span><strong>Visual Analysis, Clustering, Forecasting</strong></div>
<div><span>User guide</span><strong>Final PDF embedded on site</strong></div>
<div><span>Deployment</span><strong>shinyapps.io app linked directly from this page</strong></div>
:::
:::

::: {.landing-hero-visual}
![](guide-assets/home/clustering-dashboard-hero.png){.img-fluid}
<div class="landing-visual-note">The clustering workspace is shown here because it is the central comparison surface in the live application.</div>
:::
:::

::: {.landing-ribbon}
::: {.landing-section-head}
<div class="landing-kicker">Analytical Flow</div>
## Three stages, one coherent story

The project moves from market structure into recovery-pattern comparison and then into projection. Every page on this site supports that reading order.
:::

::: {.landing-ribbon-grid}
::: {.landing-ribbon-item}
<div class="landing-kicker">Stage 1</div>
### Market Structure

Read the Visual Analysis module first to see which source markets dominate the pre-COVID baseline and how the recovery window reshapes that ranking.
:::

::: {.landing-ribbon-item}
<div class="landing-kicker">Stage 2</div>
### Recovery Patterns

Move to the clustering workspace to compare country trajectories, identify representative recovery groups, and place one focus market back inside the cluster structure.
:::

::: {.landing-ribbon-item}
<div class="landing-kicker">Stage 3</div>
### Forward Projection

Finish in the forecasting studio to compare model families, inspect diagnostics, and explain the selected forecast with hotel and tourism context indicators.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.landing-modules}
::: {.landing-section-head}
<div class="landing-kicker">Inside The App</div>
## One Shiny workspace, three linked modules

The live application is organized as a continuous workflow instead of separate demos. Each module answers a different analytical question, and together they form one decision surface.
:::

::: {.landing-modules-grid}
::: {.landing-module}
<div class="landing-kicker">Module 1</div>
### Time Series Visual Analysis

Use the map and ranking views to identify the leading visitor markets in the selected period and to compare the pre-COVID structure with the recovery period.
:::

::: {.landing-module}
<div class="landing-kicker">Module 2</div>
### Time Series Clustering

Inspect recovery quality, representative trajectories, recovery position, focus-market placement, and exportable membership tables from one guided interface.
:::

::: {.landing-module}
<div class="landing-kicker">Module 3</div>
### Forecasting

Run one forecasting experiment at a time, compare model performance on a holdout window, and read the winning forecast together with diagnostics and context plots.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.landing-resources}
::: {.landing-section-head}
<div class="landing-kicker">Site Structure</div>
## Deliverables, prototypes, and records

The website is the documentation layer for the project. The pages below connect the final deliverables with the supporting analytical materials.
:::

::: {.landing-resources-grid}
::: {.landing-resource-column}
<div class="landing-column-title">Final deliverables</div>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./Proposal/Proposal.html">
<span>Proposal</span>
<strong>Project framing, scope, and analytical direction</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./app-guide.html">
<span>Shiny App</span>
<strong>Launch page with direct deployment link and embedded preview</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./user-guide.html">
<span>User Guide PDF</span>
<strong>Final guide embedded on site and available for download</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./poster.html">
<span>Poster</span>
<strong>Final project poster in website form</strong>
</a>
:::

::: {.landing-resource-column}
<div class="landing-column-title">Prototypes and project records</div>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./prototype/EDA.html">
<span>EDA prototype</span>
<strong>Market structure and pre-COVID versus recovery ranking views</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./prototype/CDA.html">
<span>CDA prototype</span>
<strong>Supporting comparative analysis and contextual diagnostics</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./prototype/module-cluster.html">
<span>Clustering prototype</span>
<strong>Trajectory grouping, recovery position, and cluster summaries</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./prototype/forecasting.html">
<span>Forecasting prototype</span>
<strong>Model comparison, diagnostics, and context interpretation</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./prototype/ui-storyboard.html">
<span>UI storyboard</span>
<strong>Workflow logic and interface planning across the app</strong>
</a>

<a class="landing-resource-link" href="./Meeting%20Minutes/first-meeting-record.html">
<span>Meeting records</span>
<strong>Task ownership, pivot decisions, and project history</strong>
</a>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.landing-final}
::: {.landing-final-copy}
<div class="landing-kicker">Deployment And Local Run</div>
## Launch the live app or inspect the source locally

The homepage button opens the deployed Shiny application directly. The PDF guide explains the intended reading order, and the same sequence is preserved inside the website.
:::

::: {.landing-final-actions}
[Launch Deployed Shiny App](`r shiny_app_url`){target="_blank" .btn-home-primary}
[Open User Guide PDF](./user-guide.html){.btn-home-secondary}
:::

::: {.landing-runtime}
<div><span>Local website</span><strong>`quarto preview`</strong></div>
<div><span>Local Shiny app</span><strong>`Rscript run_app.R 3838`</strong></div>
<div><span>Shared data backbone</span><strong>`data/raw/visitor_arrivals_full_dataset.xlsx`</strong></div>
:::
:::
:::
